openmeter/subscription/addon/diff.purpose: Pure-functional engine that computes how a SubscriptionAddon transforms a SubscriptionSpec (apply) and how to undo it (restore). It bridges subscription addons into the spec model by splitting/merging items per phase so the addon's rate cards are present for its cadence; constraint: this package is internal to subscription and never persists.

patterns.name: Diffable apply/restore pair
patterns.description: Every addon transform is expressed as a Diffable with GetApplies() and GetRestores() returning subscription.AppliesToSpec; the two operations must be exact inverses (restore_test asserts SpecsEqual after apply+restore).
patterns.example: type Diffable interface { GetApplies() subscription.AppliesToSpec; GetRestores() subscription.AppliesToSpec }

patterns.name: AppliesToSpec closures, never direct mutation
patterns.description: Spec changes are wrapped in subscription.NewAppliesToSpec(func(spec *SubscriptionSpec, actx ApplyContext) error {...}) and aggregated via subscription.NewAggregateAppliesToSpec; callers invoke spec.Apply(...). Do not mutate the spec outside an AppliesToSpec.

patterns.name: Per-phase gap/intersection splitting
patterns.description: getApplyForRateCard walks spec.GetSortedPhases(), intersects the addon cadence with each phase via timeutil.OpenPeriod, tracks uncovered gaps, splits existing items into difference+intersection parts, and creates new items for gaps using rc.Apply Quantity times.

patterns.name: Quantity-driven rate card application
patterns.description: rc.Apply(rateCard, annotations) is invoked exactly d.addon.Quantity times (gaps use Quantity-1 since the base card already exists); Quantity==0 yields a no-op AppliesToSpec. Merge math lives in subscriptionaddon, not here.
patterns.example: for range d.addon.Quantity { err := rc.Apply(inst.RateCard, inst.Annotations) }

patterns.name: Relative cadence via setItemRelativeCadence
patterns.description: Item cadences are stored as ActiveFromOverrideRelativeToPhaseStart / ActiveToOverrideRelativeToPhaseStart durations computed with datetime.ISODurationBetween(phaseStart, target); overrides equal to the phase boundary are left nil.

patterns.name: Restore deletes only effectively-zero items
patterns.description: restore() undoes rc.Restore Quantity times, then deletes an item only if zeroRateCardCheck.CanDelete() (zero price/entitlement AND not owned by OwnerSubscriptionSubSystem), and re-merges subsequent identical items.
patterns.example: chk := zeroRateCardCheck{itemAnnotations: item.Annotations, rc: target}; if chk.CanDelete() { rmIdxs = append(rmIdxs, idx) }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Diffable interface and someDiffable (closure-backed Apply/Restore fns)
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetApplies/GetRestores must wrap fns in NewAppliesToSpec; keep the two symmetric

key_file_guides.role: GetDiffableFromAddon builds a someDiffable from an addon's instances; per-instance diffable aggregates per-rate-card applies
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns (nil, nil) when no instances/quantities — callers must nil-check the Diffable (see create.go list flow)
Gaps use Quantity-1 applies; phases iterated only between phaseAtCadenceStart and phaseAtCadenceEnd; sort newItems by cadence

key_file_guides.role: Inverse of apply: undo rc effects, delete zero items, re-merge subsequent identical items
key_file_guides.watch_for: Merge predicate compares RateCard.Equal, Annotations DeepEqual, BillingBehaviorOverride, and subsequency; empty ItemsByKey entries are deleted

key_file_guides.role: zeroRateCardCheck.CanDelete — decides if a restored item is safe to remove
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only measures fields rc.Apply/Restore touches (Price, EntitlementTemplate); non-flat price or owner annotation blocks deletion

key_file_guides.role: GetAffectedItemIDs maps rate-card key -> affected subscription item IDs (used by http mapping)
key_file_guides.watch_for: Marked FIXME for placement; uses AddonRateCardMatcherForAGivenPlanRateCard and dedupes with lo.Uniq

anti_patterns: Mutating SubscriptionSpec phases/items outside a NewAppliesToSpec closure
Making GetApplies and GetRestores non-inverse (breaks restore_test SpecsEqual assertions)
Calling rc.Apply a number of times other than addon.Quantity (gaps use Quantity-1)
Persisting or reaching into repos/services from this package — it is pure spec math, internal to subscription
Setting absolute item cadences instead of relative ActiveFrom/ActiveToOverrideRelativeToPhaseStart durations

decisions.decision: Express addon application as reversible Diffable apply/restore rather than mutating persisted state
decisions.rationale: Lets the workflow layer compose addon changes onto an in-memory spec and undo them deterministically; tests verify symmetry

decisions.decision: Item splitting/merging done by period arithmetic (timeutil.OpenPeriod gaps/difference/intersection)
decisions.rationale: An addon must cover its exact cadence within each phase, possibly splitting pre-existing items; period algebra makes coverage provable

decisions.decision: RateCard merge/restore math delegated to subscriptionaddon package
decisions.rationale: Keeps this package focused on spec topology; README explicitly states merging logic lives in subscriptionaddon

openmeter/subscription/addon/http.purpose: HTTP driver (package httpdriver) for the subscription-addon REST surface: create, list, get, and update (quantity change) of subscription addons. Thin transport layer that decodes requests, delegates to the subscription-addon Service and subscription Workflow Service, and maps domain objects to api.SubscriptionAddon.

patterns.name: httptransport.HandlerWithArgs triple
patterns.description: Each endpoint defines Params/Request/Response/Handler type aliases and a method returning httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decode, handle, encode, opts...). Decode resolves namespace and decodes body; encode uses commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus.

patterns.name: Namespace resolution via resolveNamespace
patterns.description: Every decode step calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) (wraps NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace) and builds models.NamespacedID from path params; missing namespace returns a 500 HTTPError.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); SubscriptionID: models.NamespacedID{Namespace: ns, ID: params.SubscriptionID}

patterns.name: Workflow service for mutations, addon service for reads
patterns.description: Create/Update route through h.SubscriptionWorkflowService (AddAddon / ChangeAddonQuantity); Get/List read via h.SubscriptionAddonService and fetch the SubscriptionView via h.SubscriptionService.GetView for response mapping.

patterns.name: Create-or-change upsert convenience
patterns.description: CreateSubscriptionAddon lists existing addons; if the AddonID already exists it calls ChangeAddonQuantity instead of AddAddon, so POST is idempotent-by-addon.
patterns.example: if sAdd, ok := lo.Find(subsAdds.Items, ...); ok { ChangeAddonQuantity(...) } else { AddAddon(...) }

patterns.name: Response mapping requires both view and addon
patterns.description: MapSubscriptionAddonToResponse(view, addon) computes the union period of instances, current quantity at clock.Now(), affected item IDs via addondiff.GetAffectedItemIDs, and maps rate cards via productcataloghttp.FromRateCard.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface, HandlerConfig (SubscriptionAddonService, SubscriptionWorkflowService, SubscriptionService, NamespaceDecoder, Logger), NewHandler, resolveNamespace
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoints must be added to the Handler interface and wired in router

key_file_guides.role: createSubscriptionAddon — decodes SubscriptionAddonCreate, upserts via workflow service
updateSubscriptionAddon — ChangeAddonQuantity; requires Timing and Quantity in body
key_file_guides.watch_for: Explicitly errors if body.Timing or body.Quantity is nil before mapping

key_file_guides.role: Read paths; both fetch SubscriptionView via SubscriptionService.GetView to enrich the response
key_file_guides.watch_for: List uses slicesx.MapWithErr; both depend on the view being available for affected-item mapping
Errors with 'no instances found' if addon has zero instances; timing mapped via subscriptionhttp.MapAPITimingToTiming; depends on addondiff.GetAffectedItemIDs

anti_patterns: Putting business validation or persistence in handlers — delegate to Service/WorkflowService
Building NamespacedID without resolveNamespace (drops tenant scoping)
Mapping a response without the SubscriptionView (affected item IDs and union period need it)
Adding an endpoint method without registering it on the Handler interface

decisions.decision: POST create doubles as quantity-change when addon already present
decisions.rationale: Convenience so clients need not check existence first; keeps a single 'add this addon' verb

decisions.decision: Mutations go through the workflow service, not the addon service directly
decisions.rationale: AddAddon/ChangeAddonQuantity must also sync the subscription view/spec, which is the workflow layer's responsibility

openmeter/subscription/addon/repo.purpose: Ent-backed persistence (package subscriptionaddonrepo) for subscription addons and their quantity timeline. Implements subscriptionaddon.SubscriptionAddonRepository and SubscriptionAddonQuantityRepository, mapping db rows to domain types; constraint: every method is transaction-aware via entutils.TransactingRepo.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping
patterns.description: Every repo method body is wrapped in entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, r, func(ctx, repo) {...}) so it rebinds to any tx already in ctx; Tx/Self/WithTx are implemented in transaction.go.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, r, func(ctx context.Context, repo *subscriptionAddonRepo) (...) { ... })

patterns.name: Interface assertion + constructor
patterns.description: Each repo asserts the domain interface (var _ subscriptionaddon.SubscriptionAddonRepository = (*subscriptionAddonRepo)(nil)) and exposes a NewX(db *db.Client) constructor returning the unexported struct.

patterns.name: Eager-load via querySubscriptionAddon
patterns.description: Reads always go through querySubscriptionAddon which WithAddon(WithRatecards(WithFeatures, WithTaxCode)) and WithQuantities ordered by ActiveFrom then CreatedAt; mapping assumes these edges are loaded.

patterns.name: Db-to-domain mapping in mapping.go
patterns.description: MapSubscriptionAddon builds the domain SubscriptionAddon from edges; quantities become a timeutil.NewTimeline of Timed values; rate cards reuse addonrepo.FromAddonRow / FromAddonRateCardRow from productcatalog.

patterns.name: Quantity changes are append-only
patterns.description: subscriptionAddonQuantityRepo.Create inserts a new SubscriptionAddonQuantity row (ActiveFrom, Quantity); history is never mutated in place — the timeline is rebuilt on read.

patterns.name: Optional pagination
patterns.description: List returns all items as a single page when filter.Page.IsZero(), otherwise query.Paginate(ctx, filter.Page) + entutils.MapPagedWithErr; ordering selected by filter.OrderBy (ID/UpdatedAt/CreatedAt default).
patterns.example: if filter.Page.IsZero() { ... pagination.NewPage(1, len(items)) } else { query.Paginate(ctx, filter.Page) }

key_file_guides.role: subscriptionAddonRepo: Create/Get/List plus the shared querySubscriptionAddon eager-load helper
key_file_guides.watch_for: Get returns models.NewGenericNotFoundError on db.IsNotFound; List branches on Page.IsZero; mapping relies on edges loaded by querySubscriptionAddon

key_file_guides.role: subscriptionAddonQuantityRepo: append-only Create of a quantity segment
key_file_guides.watch_for: No update/delete — changing quantity means inserting a new row keyed by ActiveFrom
Reads entity.Edges.Addon.Edges.Ratecards (panics if Addon edge nil); reuses productcatalog addonrepo.FromAddonRow/FromAddonRateCardRow

key_file_guides.role: Tx/Self/WithTx for both repos via db.HijackTx + entutils.NewTxDriver
key_file_guides.watch_for: Required boilerplate for TransactingRepo to work; WithTx rebuilds the client from raw tx config

anti_patterns: Bypassing querySubscriptionAddon on reads (mapping assumes Addon/Ratecards/Quantities edges are loaded)
Updating or deleting quantity rows instead of inserting a new ActiveFrom segment
Writing a repo method without entutils.TransactingRepo wrapping
Returning raw db.IsNotFound instead of models.NewGenericNotFoundError
Hand-mapping rate cards instead of delegating to addonrepo.FromAddonRow/FromAddonRateCardRow

decisions.decision: Quantity is an append-only timeline of (ActiveFrom, Quantity) rows
decisions.rationale: Addon quantity changes over time; the domain reconstructs a timeutil.Timeline so historical quantities remain queryable

decisions.decision: Reuse productcatalog addon adapter for rate-card mapping
decisions.rationale: SubscriptionAddon rate cards are addon rate cards; sharing FromAddonRow avoids divergent mapping logic

openmeter/subscription/addon/service.purpose: Service layer (package service) implementing subscriptionaddon.Service: Create, Get, List, ChangeQuantity for subscription addons. It validates compatibility (plan linkage, phase availability, instance type, max quantity) before persisting through the repos and publishing watermill events.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate + NewService
patterns.description: Dependencies (SubAddRepo, SubAddQtyRepo, Publisher, TxManager, AddonService, PlanAddonService, SubService, Logger) are passed via a Config whose Validate() checks each non-nil; NewService(cfg) returns subscriptionaddon.Service and errors on invalid config.
patterns.example: func NewService(cfg Config) (subscriptionaddon.Service, error) { if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Validate-then-check-compatibility-then-transact
patterns.description: Create/ChangeQuantity first input.Validate() (wrapped in models.NewGenericValidationError), then fetch addon + subscription view, run compatibility checks, and only then transaction.Run(ctx, TxManager, ...) to write + publish.

patterns.name: GenericValidationError for business rules
patterns.description: Domain violations (single-instance qty != 1, addon not linked to plan, phase too early, max quantity exceeded, custom subscription) return models.NewGenericValidationError with a formatted message; tests assert via ErrorAs(&GenericValidationError{}).
patterns.example: return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("addon %s@%d can be added a maximum of %d times", add.Key, add.Version, *compatibility.MaxQuantity))

patterns.name: Phase-availability scan against PlanAddon compatibility
patterns.description: Both Create and ChangeQuantity look up planaddon.GetPlanAddon, then iterate sView.Phases stopping at compatibility.FromPlanPhase; if the addon's start phase is reached first it errors that the addon can only start from FromPlanPhase.
patterns.example: for _, phase := range sView.Phases { if phase.SubscriptionPhase.Key == compatibility.FromPlanPhase { break }; if phase...Key == phaseAtAddonStart.PhaseKey { return ...validation error } }

patterns.name: Publish customer-scoped events in-transaction
patterns.description: After persisting, the service re-fetches the addon and publishes subscriptionaddon.NewCreatedEvent / NewChangeQuantityEvent with sView.Customer via the eventbus Publisher inside the same transaction.

patterns.name: Create initial quantity alongside addon
patterns.description: Create writes the SubscriptionAddon row then the initial CreateSubscriptionAddonQuantityRepositoryInput, then re-Get to return a fully-loaded aggregate; ChangeQuantity reuses CreateSubscriptionAddonQuantityRepositoryInput(input).

key_file_guides.watch_for: Compatibility-check logic is duplicated between Create and ChangeQuantity; both re-Get after write and publish in-tx; Create blocks PlanRef==nil (custom subscriptions)

key_file_guides.role: Service-backed Create tests (invalid input, missing addon/subscription, single-instance qty, plan linkage, phase, max quantity)
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses subscriptiontestutils withDeps + createPlanWithAddon; asserts exact error strings like 'addon %s@%d is not linked to the plan'

key_file_guides.role: ChangeQuantity tests incl. max-quantity-after-purchase and single-instance guard
key_file_guides.watch_for: createExampleSubscriptionAddon helper builds plan+addon+subscription+subAddon; ChangeQuantity appends a quantity segment

key_file_guides.role: Get/List tests including pagination and name/description inheritance from addon
key_file_guides.watch_for: List returns all when Page is zero; SubscriptionAddonsEqual used for comparison

anti_patterns: Persisting before running input.Validate() and plan/phase/quantity compatibility checks
Returning plain errors for business-rule violations instead of models.NewGenericValidationError
Writing without transaction.Run, or publishing events outside the transaction
Allowing addon purchase on a subscription with nil PlanRef (custom subscription)
Skipping the re-Get before returning/publishing (event and return value must carry the full aggregate)

decisions.decision: Quantity changes are modeled as new quantity segments, not in-place edits
decisions.rationale: Mirrors the append-only repo timeline so an addon's quantity history is preserved; ChangeQuantity simply Creates another segment

decisions.decision: Compatibility (plan linkage, FromPlanPhase, MaxQuantity, instance type) is enforced in the service, not the repo
decisions.rationale: These rules depend on PlanAddon and SubscriptionView state the repo doesn't see; service is the only place with all dependencies

decisions.decision: Events published inside the same transaction as the write
decisions.rationale: Ensures created/change-quantity events are not emitted for rolled-back changes

code_examples.scenario: Validate, check compatibility, then persist + publish in a transaction
